reasoning: Merged 6 patches (4 error, 2 success) addressing spreadsheet manipulation patterns. Key overlaps identified: (1) openpyxl formula handling appears in patches 1, 4, 6 - consolidated into unified formula section with version compatibility + reference awareness; (2) file integrity from patches 1 & 5 combined; (3) iteration/range validation from patches 1, 2, 3 synthesized. Conflicts resolved by synthesizing complementary aspects (e.g., .formula vs .value use case distinction). Unique insights preserved: sheet selection, tokenized search, category normalization, sort order awareness, column deletion patterns. Resulting edits organized into logical sections while maintaining independence between concepts.

memory_items.title: openpyxl Formula Writing Best Practices
memory_items.lesson: Formula strings assigned to cell.value are stored verbatim without auto-adjustment; prefer '=FORMULA_STRING' format over .formula attribute for version compatibility
memory_items.generalization: When populating multiple cells with formulas, dynamically generate unique formula strings per target cell by interpolating row/column indices rather than reusing static formula templates

memory_items.title: Source File Protection Protocol
memory_items.lesson: Never modify original workbooks in-place; always create backup copies before editing operations
memory_items.generalization: Use filesystem duplication operations prior to any workbook modifications, ensuring the source remains untouched regardless of operation success or failure

memory_items.title: Absolute vs Relative Reference Discipline
memory_items.lesson: Lock lookup table references with absolute notation ($A$1:$A$4) while preserving relative positioning for dynamic lookup keys ($A14)
memory_items.generalization: Audit formula reference types before batch-writing formulas to confirm expected behavior matches actual data retrieval logic across fill ranges

memory_items.title: Multi-Sheet Workbook Access
memory_items.lesson: Verify wb.sheetnames programmatically and select target sheets by name (wb['SheetName']) instead of assuming wb.active contains the data
memory_items.generalization: Always enumerate available worksheets and explicitly reference the correct sheet before any read/write operations in multi-sheet workbooks

memory_items.title: Iterative Range Application Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Validate actual row counts against expectations before iteration to prevent off-by-one errors or unintended row inclusion/exclusion
memory_items.generalization: Use programmatic loops to apply consistent formula logic across contiguous cell ranges rather than coding each cell individually

memory_items.title: String Category Normalization
memory_items.lesson: Inspect unique values in pivot/aggregation columns before execution to detect plural/singular variations, typos, or case differences
memory_items.generalization: Before pivot_table() calls, verify all source values map to expected target names and use reindex() to handle missing categories with appropriate fill values

memory_items.title: Tokenized Text Search Strategy
memory_items.lesson: Split free-text comment fields on common delimiters (/, -, space, |, comma) and compare normalized tokens rather than using plain substring containment
memory_items.generalization: For parameter extraction from variable-format text fields, preprocess both search terms and target text by normalizing delimiters before comparison

memory_items.title: Date Format Handling Robustness
memory_items.lesson: Process dates stored as datetime objects, strings, or serial numbers with type checking and try/except parse fallbacks
memory_items.generalization: Implement multi-format conversion with graceful degradation to handle inconsistent temporal data sources without workflow interruption

memory_items.title: Cascading Logic Sort Order Verification
memory_items.lesson: Confirm dataset sort direction aligns with intended fill patterns (fill above/below matching rows) before implementing conditional cascading formulas
memory_items.generalization: Newest-to-oldest vs oldest-to-newest ordering reverses implied direction of multi-row conditional logic operations

memory_items.title: Output Directory Preparation
memory_items.lesson: Create output directories programmatically with exist_ok=True before saving modified workbooks
memory_items.generalization: Ensure target output paths exist using platform-independent utilities before attempting any file write operations

edits.content:
## 6. Core Manipulation Patterns
- **Formula Writing**: Use `cell.value = '=FORMULA_STRING'` instead of `.formula` attribute for maximum openpyxl compatibility. Generate unique formula strings per target cell via template interpolation rather than reusing static formulas.
- **Reference Anchoring**: Lock lookup table references with absolute notation ($A$1:$B$4) while keeping lookup keys relative ($A14) for proper fill-range behavior.
- **File Integrity**: Create backup copies before any modifications; never alter the original workbook in-place.
- **Safe Column Removal**: Shift all columns right of deletion index leftward (preserving styles), then set trailing column values to None to prevent ghost data.

## 7. Workbook Navigation & Validation
- **Explicit Sheet Selection**: Enumerate `wb.sheetnames` and access worksheets by name (`wb['SheetName']`) rather than relying on `wb.active`.
- **Row Range Verification**: Log actual `ws.max_row` values before iteration and cross-reference with specification requirements to prevent off-by-one errors.
- **Category Inspection**: Before pivoting, print unique values from category columns to detect plural/singular variations, whitespace issues, or case mismatches.

## 8. Data Processing Best Practices
- **Tokenized Matching**: For parameters embedded in compound strings (e.g., 'HU/table'), split on common delimiters (/,-,space,|,comma) and compare normalized tokens.
- **Date Format Resilience**: Implement type checking with try/except blocks to handle datetime objects, strings, and serial numbers gracefully.
- **Sort Direction Awareness**: Verify ascending/descending order before implementing cascading fill patterns that depend on row sequencing.
- **Logging Visibility**: Print intermediate calculations (thresholds computed, rows processed) to enable debugging and result verification.

changelog_entries: Consolidated 6 patches into unified spreadsheet skill documentation
Deduplicated openpyxl formula guidance from 3 patches into single comprehensive section
Merged file integrity principles (patches 1+5) with safe column removal pattern
Added category normalization and tokenized search strategies from error analysis patches
Preserved unique insights on sort order awareness and date handling robustness
Organized edits into 3 logical sections: Core Patterns, Navigation, Data Processing
